displayTitle: Everything Leaks — The Question Is How Much

beats.narration: No pressure boundary is absolutely tight. Every weld, seal, and fitting leaks something. Leak testing never asks whether it leaks — only how much.

payload.title: EVERYTHING LEAKS
payload.sub: The only question is whether it stays under the allowable rate.

beats.narration: Four fundamental techniques cover the working range. Bubbles make a leak visible. A gauge totals it. Halogen sniffers chase refrigerant. Helium reaches the finest leaks of all.

payload.heading: Four ways to find a leak

beats.narration: Stack them on a scale and the spread is enormous. Bubbles see down near ten to the minus five; helium total-leakage tests reach ten to the minus eleven.

payload.heading: The sensitivity ladder spans a million-fold
payload.note: EACH ADDED DECADE COSTS TIME, CLEANLINESS, AND SKILL

beats.narration: Two questions decide it. Must the answer locate the leak, or total the whole boundary? And which technique clears the allowable rate — with margin, and no more?

payload.heading: How the right technique is chosen

chips.s: Find the fix, or prove compliance

beats.narration: So the specification picks the technique. Start from the maximum allowable rate, choose what just clears it, and prove the number against a calibrated standard leak.

payload.sub: The allowable rate picks the technique — sensitivity beyond it buys cost, not quality.
